headline: Co-Curricular Activities Consume RM1.8 Billion But Lack Outcome Data
context: Schools spend RM1.8 billion annually on co-curricular programs with no systematic measurement of student development outcomes.

cards.big: RM1.8 billion spent annually on school co-curricular activities with zero outcome measurement
cards.sub: Participation is compulsory but developmental impact is never assessed

cards.big: Students must participate in minimum 3 co-curricular activities earning 10% of school grade
cards.sub: Grade allocation creates compliance incentive but not genuine engagement

cards.big: Only 12% of schools track student skill development through co-curricular participation
cards.sub: Activities are recorded as attendance not as measurable competency building

cards.big: Hong Kong's Other Learning Experiences framework measures 5 competencies through co-curricular
cards.sub: Structured outcome assessment transforms activities from filler to development tool

cards.big: Co-curricular activities without outcome measurement are expensive free time not development programs
Should co-curricular programs measure and report specific student development outcomes
